summary: mathepy silently relays user prompts to author's Groq account via hardcoded API keys
details: Package describes itself as a 'Module for Quick Calculations' but its public API (ask_llm, pink, america, iran, momo, dropnull, code, sf, abc, liti, bcd, init, lc, koko) instantiates a Groq LLM client with hardcoded API keys and forwards the caller-supplied prompt to api.groq.com. For example, src/mathepy/ai_helper.py line 4 contains `client = Groq(api_key="gsk_...")` followed by `messages=[{"role":"user","content":prompt}]`. At least 13 distinct hardcoded Groq keys are spread across ai_helper.py, init.py, abc.py, pink.py, america.py, iran.py, momo.py, dropnull.py, code.py, sf.py, liti.py, bcd.py, lc.py, and koko.py. Any caller of these advertised functions has their prompt content routed through and logged under the author's Groq account, which the installer never consented to. Package metadata is placeholder (fakehu / fakehu45@gmail.com) and the description does not mention LLM relay. The shipped keys themselves only consume the author's quota (author self-harm), but the silent off-host data flow of caller-supplied input through the package's advertised API is the installer-side harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
